1 - This movie, and the next, are tweaked based on fan reception to the prior. It'll likely have more conventionally scary action at the minimum.

2 - This isn't the games continuity, this movie is its own thing.

3 - Not only is FNaF Plus not an adaptation, it wasn't even made by Scott, nor was it even finished.

4 - TJOC is barely a FNaF adaptation. Its closer to a meta-supernatural horror about creators and their creations, filled with shapeshifting demons. The story can stand firm even if the FNaF characters are swapped out.

I think it's interesting that Scott Cawthon is credited as the sole writer of this one. He had writing credits on the previous one, but I assumed it was more in line with Scott's previous works where he'd outsource the bulk of the writing to authors and collaborate with them, which would make sense with Emma Tammi, Seth Cuddeback, Chris Lee Hill, and Tyler Macintyre being credited for the first film's screenplay and screenstory along with Scott.

This teaser seems to attribute Scott as the sole author of the film. Unless, of course, they're just truncating the credits for the sake of the teaser, and the actual trailer and/or film will reveal the whole list of names associated with the project. However, if this film is written solely by Scott, I think it'll be interesting to see Scott's style of writing across the whole duration of the film. Scott's really great at writing aura and hype moments, but I'd be curious how good he'd be at actually penning a real 90+ minute film, including all the non-hype stuff, like one-on-one interactions, dialogue, etc.

After seeing the trailer, I think from what I saw is that there are many improvements done from the previous movie, putting killer animatronics in a city rather than being stuck in a small pizzeria gives it more thrill as you don't know what will happen, the problem with the first movie is that it wasn't scary cause you knew when the danger was coming. Just like Pewdiepie once said you get used to it since your mind knows that if Mike is in the pizzeria then he will probably be jumpscared. But now you don't know what will happen now that you know a jumpscare could happen anywhere in any time but you won't know. This makes it way scarier than the first movie already, not only that there is also way more animatronics than before, and there is also the Withered Animatronics yet to be seen, making the whole movie a mystery waiting to be uncovered while waiting for you to pick the pieces to the story. Which makes it similar to those spooky story books you read back in the day when it was halloween along with your friends, it wasn't all scary, it felt more like an adventure filled with action which is why I think this movie will probably shine better than the previous movie only if the acting is good. We will just have to see.
